key_insight: A repeatable two-part silicone pour-casting workflow can produce soft pneumatic actuators with reliable internal cavities and embedded flex sensing.
engineering_relevance: The procedures improve manufacturability and calibration of soft robotic actuators, making them more practical for scalable deployment in rehabilitation and surgical systems.
method: Robust pour-casting steps to prevent cavity clogging and ensure airtight sealing.
Sensor embedding procedure for thin-film flex sensors in the actuator body.
FEM plus PID-controlled pneumatic experiments to compare simulated and physical bending response.
evidence_signal: The paper validates the workflow across two operators and 24 successful fabrications, with staircase and sinusoidal tests showing repeatable actuation and close agreement between simulated and measured bending angles.
watch_item: Viscoelastic effects introduce some hysteresis under dynamic sinusoidal actuation, so calibration may need to be maintained across material batches and operating conditions.
paper_title: Robust Silicone Pour Casting and Sensor Embedding Procedures for Soft Robotic Actuators
